page.content: The data includes sex, age in years, percentage predicted FEV1, the allergen inhaled, and the titration of inhaled allergen. The first row, labeled "M," indicates a male aged 61 with a predicted FEV1 of 96%, inhaling a cat allergen at a titration of 1:32. The second row, labeled "F," represents a female aged 24 with a predicted FEV1 of 80%, exposed to ragweed at a titration of 1:16.

page.content: The third row, another male aged 52, has a predicted FEV1 of 95%, inhaling house dust mite (HDM) at a titration of 1:64. The fourth row shows a female aged 40 with a predicted FEV1 of 104%, inhaling a cat allergen at a titration of 1:128. The fifth row presents a male aged 25 with a predicted FEV1 of 86%, exposed to ragweed at a titration of 1:8. The sixth row features a 24-year-old female with a predicted FEV1 of 93%, inhaling HDM at a titration of 1:8. The seventh row identifies a male aged 26 with a predicted FEV1 of 93%, exposed to HDM at a titration of 1:32. The eighth row indicates a 20-year-old male with a predicted FEV1 of 94%, inhaling grass at a titration of 1:4. The ninth row presents a female aged 34 with a predicted FEV1 of 70%, inhaling a cat allergen at a titration of 1:512. The tenth row shows a male aged 26 with a predicted FEV1 of 96%, exposed to a cat allergen at a titration of 1:8. The overall distribution of participants is 6 males and 4 females, with a mean age of 33.2 ± 13.6 years and a mean predicted FEV1 of 90.7 ± 9.6%.
